title: Young Conquers Sawgrass: Players Championship Glory
excerpt: Cameron Young birdied the iconic 17th and drilled a 375-yard bomb on 18 to claim his second PGA Tour title at TPC Sawgrass.
body:
The Final Round Drama

Cameron Young learned how to get out of his own way. The 28-year-old stopped stressing over unrealistic goals and produced some of the finest golf of his life down the stretch at TPC Sawgrass. Trailing by four strokes entering Sunday, Young birdied the iconic island-green 17th and tapped in for par at the treacherous 18th to shoot a closing 68 and win The Players Championship by one stroke over Matt Fitzpatrick.

"What kept me going all week is honestly, I pretty much walked down the fairways looking at my feet asking myself where my feet were," Young said.

Winner
Cameron Young (-13)
Runner-up
Matt Fitzpatrick (-12)
Third
Xander Schauffele (-11)
Drive on 18
375 yards

The Collapse and the Charge

54-hole leader Ludvig Aberg appeared poised to claim golf's richest prize, but the Stadium Course had other ideas. The Swede rinsed his second shot at the par-5 11th and followed that by hooking his drive into the water at the short par-4 12th, racking up a double-bogey six that derailed his challenge.

While Aberg unravelled, Fitzpatrick and Young pounced. The Englishman birdied three holes in a four-hole stretch beginning at the 12th, including a 12-footer at the 15th to take the lead.

The Shot of His Life

Standing on the 18th tee tied with Fitzpatrick, Young faced the same water hazard that had swallowed his tee shot on Saturday. His mental coach Bhrett McCabe had predicted that Saturday mistake would ultimately win him the tournament.

"My thought process over that ball is, one, making sure that I'm committed to my line, and two, the overarching thought is, 'I'm going to hit the best shot of my life right here,'" Young said. He delivered a 375-yard blast into the fairway, the longest drive on 18 in the ShotLink era since 2004.

"I drove it so well last week, and I drove it so average this week. But that one on 18, I don't know if I can think of one that's better."

The Road Ahead

This victory marks Young's second PGA Tour title, coming after his breakthrough at the Wyndham Championship in August 2025 following seven runner-up finishes in 93 starts.

The PGA Tour now heads to the Texas Children's Houston Open before the Valero Texas Open in San Antonio, the final opportunity for players to qualify for Augusta. Then all eyes turn to Georgia for the Masters Tournament, April 9-12, where Young will look to carry this momentum onto the game's grandest stage.

Apr 9-12
Masters Tournament
Augusta National
